name: openwork ui mcp tool-selection matrix
description: Compare docs-level and source-level descriptions for OpenWork UI-control MCP routing.

source.slice: UI status, snapshot, available actions, semantic action execution, and no-tool boundaries

instruction_variants.name: openwork_ui_host_rules
instruction_variants.instructions: Choose the single OpenWork UI MCP tool that should be called next. The tool surface controls semantic OpenWork actions through a local desktop bridge. If no OpenWork UI-control tool can safely perform the next step, choose NO_TOOL.

tools.name: ui_status
tools.purpose: Check if OpenWork is running and reachable. Returns connection status and app info.

tools.name: ui_snapshot
tools.purpose: Get current UI state: route, narration, visible actions, and status.

tools.name: ui_list_actions
tools.purpose: List all UI control actions available. Each action id can be passed to ui_execute_action.

tools.name: ui_execute_action
tools.purpose: Execute an OpenWork UI action by id.

tools.name: ui_status
tools.purpose: Check whether the OpenWork desktop app and local UI-control bridge are reachable.
tools.use_when: Use for connectivity checks, app-not-running uncertainty, or before diagnosing bridge errors.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user needs the current route, visible actions, transcript, or screen state. Use ui_snapshot for current UI state.
quality_checks: Use before trying to execute actions when the user says OpenWork may not be running.

tools.name: ui_snapshot
tools.purpose: Read the current OpenWork route, narration, visible actions, busy status, and on-screen state.
tools.use_when: Use before taking an action when the current UI state is unknown, when the user asks what screen they are on, or when you need visible action context.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for a pure connectivity check. Avoid when the user already provided the exact action id and arguments to execute.
quality_checks: Start with a snapshot before acting on unknown UI state.

tools.name: ui_list_actions
tools.purpose: List executable semantic OpenWork UI actions, including action ids and required arguments.
tools.use_when: Use when you need to discover valid action ids or required args before executing.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the current screen state is the main unknown. Use ui_snapshot first for route and visible actions.
quality_checks: Use before ui_execute_action when action id or args are not known.

tools.name: ui_execute_action
tools.purpose: Execute a known semantic OpenWork UI action by id through the local bridge.
tools.use_when: Use only when the action id is known from ui_list_actions or the user provided a valid action id, with required args available.
tools.avoid_when: Avoid for coordinate clicks, direct DOM operations, unknown action ids, unknown required args, or when OpenWork bridge reachability is unknown.
quality_checks: Use semantic actions rather than coordinate clicks.
Check status first if the app may not be running.

cases.name: bridge check uses status
cases.task: I am not sure whether OpenWork is running. Check whether the desktop bridge is reachable.

cases.name: unknown current screen uses snapshot
cases.task: Before doing anything in OpenWork, tell me what screen I am on and which visible actions are available.

cases.name: action discovery uses list actions
cases.task: List every OpenWork UI action id I can execute and show the arguments each one needs.

cases.name: known action id executes action
cases.task: Execute the known OpenWork UI action composer.submit with args {"confirm": true}.

cases.name: unknown action id lists actions first
cases.task: Type the text 'Draft the customer note' into the OpenWork composer, but I do not know the action id or argument shape.

cases.name: coordinate click avoids semantic bridge
cases.task: Click pixel coordinate 400,300 in OpenWork. Do not use a semantic action id.

cases.name: app maybe closed checks status before action
cases.task: OpenWork may have quit, but if it is running I want to send the current composer. Check the bridge before doing anything else.
